steps.title: Identify the signal about variety
steps.content:
The passage opens with folk and traditional arts taking “a wide variety of forms.” This signals the blank should express meaningful differences.

Storytelling and quilting are presented as specific examples—and they are fundamentally unlike each other (one narrative, one visual)—proving the stated variety.

steps.title: Complete the comparative structure
steps.content:
The phrase “as different from one another” completes the comparative structure and directly supports the opening claim: folk traditions include forms as fundamentally unlike each other as storytelling and quilting.

The other choices (useful to, humble about, confused with) create illogical relationships.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
